---
title: "5.1.4 Web browsers"
subtitle: "The internet and the World Wide Web · P182-P183"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .figure-placeholder { border: 1px dashed #8aa0bf; border-radius: 8px; background: #f4f8ff; color: #40526b; padding: 1.1em 1.3em; margin: 0.8em 0; font-size: 0.9em; text-align: center; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.74em; line-height: 1.1; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.24em 0.34em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)

## Browser Purpose

### Access and display web pages

Browsers are software that allow a user to <span class="mark">access and display web pages</span> on their device screens.

Browsers interpret or translate the <span class="term">HTML</span> from websites and show the result of the translation, for example videos, images/text and audio.

## Browser Features

### Pages and navigation

Most browsers have the following features:

::: {.tight-list}
- they have a <span class="term">home page</span>
- they can store a user's favourite websites or web pages as <span class="term">bookmarks</span>
- they keep a <span class="term">history</span> of websites visited by the user
- they allow the user to navigate forwards and backwards through opened web pages
:::

## Browser Features

### Tabs, links and data

Browsers also:

::: {.tight-list}
- allow many web pages to be open at the same time using <span class="term">multiple tabs</span>
- make use of <span class="term">cookies</span>
- make use of <span class="term">hyperlinks</span> that allow navigation between websites and web pages
- store data as a <span class="term">cache</span>
:::

## Hyperlinks

### Opening links

Hyperlinks can be opened in one of two ways:

::: {.tight-list}
- open in a new tab by using `<ctrl> + <click>`
- open in the same tab by simply clicking on the link
:::

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 5.1 Hyperlink example screenshot placeholder
:::

## Address Bar

### Browser interface

Browsers make use of <span class="term">JavaScript</span> and use an <span class="term">address bar</span>.

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 5.2 Browser address bar screenshot placeholder
:::

## Classroom Check

### Browser features

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- browsers access and display web pages on device screens
- browsers interpret HTML and show videos, images/text and audio
- bookmarks, user history and tabs are common browser features
- hyperlinks support navigation between websites and web pages
- browsers use cookies, cache, JavaScript and an address bar
:::

## Bridge

### Next: Retrieving web pages

The next deck explains how DNS helps a browser locate a web server.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)
